title: Root Lifecycle Fixture Matrix
goal: Add root-level synthetic fixture coverage for lifecycle behavior across realistic host-repo states.
non_goals: Do not make destructive lifecycle operations automatic.
Do not exhaustively preserve every historical product shape when later lanes may remove or refuse them.

intent.outcome: Root lifecycle commands have black-box coverage for the main host-repo states in issue #481.
intent.constraints: Use root agentic-workspace commands and preserve package boundaries.
intent.latitude: Add focused test helpers and compact coverage metadata where it clarifies the safety contract.
intent.escalation: Stop if the fixture matrix requires product behavior outside #481.

execution.next_step: Implement tests and coverage payload updates for root lifecycle fixture states.
execution.proof: Run focused workspace CLI lifecycle tests, proof selector, and workflow closeout checks.

invariants: Root commands remain the lifecycle front door.
Local-only state is preserved by default.
Ambiguous ownership requires review before mutation.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make lifecycle management straightforward, predictable, and low-judgment.

required_continuation.follow-on detail: #482, #483, and #484 remain after this fixture matrix lands.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Lifecycle changes can be reviewed against a root synthetic matrix instead of ad hoc scenarios.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: The proof selector correctly flags direct root CLI edits for authority review; this edit stayed in derived lifecycle safety metadata rather than command/interface semantics.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Root lifecycle fixture matrix tests pass and lifecycle safety output advertises the requested state classes.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Reingest and repeat until out of issues.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Continue the open issue roadmap lane-by-lane using the package workflow.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Implement #481 as the next open lifecycle hardening child.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that tests cover the requested fixture classes without turning product residue into new default surfaces.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Root CLI lifecycle implementation/tests plus planning execplan/state.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused pytest, proof selector, ruff, workflow closeout commands.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Stop before broad CLI lifecycle rewrites or module package internals.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Branch switching, destructive cleanup, or package-local lifecycle behavior unrelated to the root matrix.

stop_conditions.stop when: Focused lifecycle fixture coverage and closeout proof pass.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The work requires broad lifecycle rewrites or destructive behavior changes.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: A follow-up belongs to #482, #483, or #484 instead of #481.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: A lifecycle fixture exposes an actual behavior regression outside this slice.

context_budget.live working set: Issue #481, root lifecycle tests, lifecycle safety payload, and active execplan.
context_budget.recoverable later: Full historical archive and unrelated derived follow-up candidates.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update this execplan with proof, closure, and continuation status.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: agentic-workspace summary and workflow_obligations report were used.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Not needed; this is a root lifecycle test slice.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Continue by running focused lifecycle tests and proof selector.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before commit or if proof fails after implementation.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Implement #481 end-to-end with root lifecycle fixture coverage.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Stay on the current branch, commit after proof, and keep lifecycle authority at the root command.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact helper shape, coverage metadata wording, and focused test grouping.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The fixture matrix requires destructive behavior changes or broad module rewrites.

active_milestone.scope: Root lifecycle tests and compact fixture coverage metadata.
active_milestone.ready: Issue #481 is open and current summary is clean.

immediate_next_action: Archive this plan, commit the milestone, close #481, refresh external evidence, then promote #482.
invariants: Root lifecycle commands stay black-box from the host repo perspective.
validation_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q -k "root_lifecycle_fixture_matrix or lifecycle_safety_payload_advertises_root_fixture_matrix or upgrade_lifecycle_plan_advertises_root_front_door"
uv run ruff check src tests

uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed tests/test_workspace_cli.py src/agentic_workspace/cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/root-lifecycle-fixture-matrix.plan.json --format json
uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json
uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
uv run agentic-workspace report --target . --section closeout_trust --format json
uv run agentic-workspace skills --target . --task "dogfooding closeout review" --format json
completion_criteria: Fixture classes requested by #481 have root-level tests or explicit coverage metadata.
Upgrade dry-run/apply covers memory-only, planning-only, and full installed states.
Local-only state preservation and ambiguous ownership review remain covered.
Optional legacy surfaces are classified rather than silently ignored.

execution_run.what happened: Added root lifecycle fixture matrix tests for empty, routing-only, partial, memory-only, planning-only, full, legacy residue, local-only, and ambiguous ownership states; exposed the fixture matrix in lifecycle mutation-safety output.
execution_run.scope touched: Root workspace CLI lifecycle safety metadata, workspace CLI tests, and checked-in planning surfaces.
execution_run.changed surfaces: preflight, summary, report, proof, reconcile, doctor, skills, root_cli_authority defaults, maintainer-surfaces.
execution_run.validations run: focused lifecycle pytest; full tests/test_workspace_cli.py; ruff; proof selector; root CLI authority; planning doctor; planning surface check; maintainer-surfaces; preflight; summary; closeout_trust report; reconcile; skills dogfooding query.
execution_run.result for continuation: #481 is ready to close; lifecycle-management-hardening continues with #482, #483, and #484.
execution_run.next step: Archive plan, commit, push, close #481, refresh external evidence, then continue #482.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; changes stayed in root lifecycle tests, compact lifecycle safety metadata, and planning records.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; root lifecycle fixture classes requested by #481 now have black-box tests or explicit coverage metadata.
finished_run_review.config compliance: used package workflow surfaces and stayed on the current branch.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; no destructive behavior was made automatic.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: close #481 and continue lifecycle hardening with #482.

proof_report.validation proof: 197 workspace CLI tests passed; ruff passed; proof selector, root CLI authority, planning doctor, planning surface check, maintainer-surfaces, preflight, closeout_trust, reconcile, and skills query completed successfully.
proof_report.proof achieved now: Root lifecycle fixture matrix coverage is present and validated.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: #481 Add root synthetic lifecycle fixture matrix.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The matrix covers empty, routing-only, memory-only, planning-only, full, old current-memory, old optional planning, custom AGENTS.md, partial managed, local-only, and ambiguous ownership states; upgrade dry-run/status/doctor are tested for memory-only, planning-only, and full installs.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Keep the product streamlined and low-judgment while preserving leverage.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Prefer compact tests and coverage signals over new user-facing surfaces.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Does lifecycle management become easier to trust without adding first-contact residue?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The bounded slice is complete and remaining intent is routed to a checked-in continuation owner.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Prepared closeout records intent satisfaction, closure decision, proof evidence, and distillation buckets.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen when .agentic-workspace/planning/state.toml roadmap lane lifecycle-management-hardening activates a fresh bounded slice.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Root synthetic lifecycle fixture matrix added for #481.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: Focused and full workspace CLI tests, ruff, proof selector, root CLI authority, planning doctor, planning surface check, maintainer-surfaces, preflight, closeout_trust, reconcile, and skills dogfooding query passed.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): No new durable memory/docs/config promotion needed; direct CLI authority friction is already covered by #410.

signals found: Proof selector flagged direct CLI edits for authority review; existing root_cli_authority defaults and #410 cover the durable follow-up.
signals routed: #410 remains the extraction/guard owner for broader direct CLI authority work.
signals dismissed: No new issue needed for this slice because the edit was derived lifecycle safety metadata and the existing authority review path classified it.
improvement_signal_review.next owner: #410 for future interface extraction/guard work; #482 for next lifecycle lane.

discard.summary: One-off command output and temporary fixture debugging.

memory.summary: No new durable memory/docs/config promotion needed; direct CLI authority friction is already covered by #410.
